text:
Modern public policymaking is increasingly shaped by the opportunity to leverage vast "big data" streams—from e-services and mobile apps to social media—into evidence-based decisions. While Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) provide the computational power necessary to automate and scale the analysis of these datasets, their integration into the public sector is fraught with technical, political, and operational hurdles. Crucially, AI-driven policies must not only be accurate but also transparent and explainable to be trusted by officials and the public, all while complying with strict new regulatory frameworks like the European Union’s AI Act.

This paper addresses these challenges by introducing a comprehensive technological ecosystem designed for trustworthy policymaking. Our contributions include: (i) a reference architecture specifically designed for AI-based policy development; (ii) a virtualized, cloud-based platform—the Virtual Policy Management Environment (VPME)—that facilitates the collaborative specification and implementation of data-driven policies; (iii) a machine learning framework capable of generating transparent, rule-based explanations for "black box" models; and (iv) practical guidelines for aligning these technical solutions with legal regulatory requirements. We conclude by demonstrating the real-world utility of these tools through various pilot cases involving local government operations, such as smart parking and infrastructure management.
